key_findings: RAS systems integration is highly consolidated — AKVA Group dominant, Billund Aquaculture bankruptcy reduces competition further. Cermaq already contracted with AKVA (€60M, Hasvik).
SCADA & automation carries the highest lock-in risk in the category — proprietary systems from AKVA/ScaleAQ create long-term switching costs. Negotiate open architecture from day one.
HVAC & ventilation for RAS is a specialist niche — fewer than 5 credible vendors in Norway. Munters leads globally; early engagement critical due to 6–9 month lead times.
Oxygen supply is a recurring cost category often underweighted at build stage — sole-source contracts with AGA or Air Liquide are common but carry volume risk.
Civil construction capacity in Northern Norway (Finnmark/Nordland) is constrained — major contractors (AF Gruppen, NCC) are in high demand for energy projects.
executive_summary.recommendation: Dual-source critical categories. Negotiate open-architecture SCADA. Lock in HVAC and oxygen early.

categories.name: RAS Systems Integration
categories.description: Turnkey recirculating aquaculture system design, supply, install and commission

tier1.name: AKVA Group
tier1.strength: World's largest aquaculture tech supplier. Full turnkey RAS. Already contracted with Cermaq (Hasvik €60M).

tier1.name: Pure Salmon Technology
tier1.strength: 15+ years, 20+ facilities including world's largest (Helgeland Smolt, Lerøy Sjøtroll). Strong Norwegian track record.

tier1.name: ScaleAQ
tier1.strength: Full-range RAS + digital/software. Strong closed aquaculture systems (CAS) portfolio.

tier2.name: MAT-KULING
tier2.note: Norwegian manufacturer, 'world's only one-stop shop' for water treatment equipment. Strong component supplier.

tier2.name: North AquaCulture
tier2.note: Founded 2021 by RAS experts. Newer player, growing. Good for component/subsystem work.

tier2.name: Nofitech
tier2.note: RAS systems with strong academic/research links. Smaller scale.

excluded.name: Billund Aquaculture
excluded.reason: BANKRUPT 2024 — AKVA Group has taken over some of their projects. Critical risk flag for any existing Billund installations.

categories.lock_in_flag: AKVA's RAS software and control systems are proprietary. Switching post-installation is costly (est. 6–18 months, EUR 2–5M). Negotiate open-architecture and data portability at contract stage.

categories.name: Civil & Building Construction
categories.description: Facility construction — structural, civil engineering, concrete works, building envelope for 15,000–25,000 m² facility

tier1.name: AF Gruppen
tier1.strength: Major Norwegian contractor with industrial/aquaculture experience. Strong in Northern Norway.

tier1.name: NCC Norway
tier1.strength: Large-scale industrial construction, proven aquaculture facility experience.
tier1.risk: Long lead times; prioritises large contracts (>NOK 100M)

tier1.name: Hæhre Entreprenør
tier1.strength: Strong Northern Norway presence, civil and industrial works.

tier2.name: Peab Bygg
tier2.note: Scandinavian contractor, strong concrete and industrial works capability.

tier2.name: Consto
tier2.note: Mid-size Norwegian contractor, growing aquaculture portfolio.

categories.lock_in_flag: None. Civil construction is commoditised — competitive tendering recommended.

categories.name: HVAC & Ventilation
categories.description: Specialised aquaculture HVAC — humidity control, CO2/H2S ventilation, temperature management, energy recovery for RAS buildings

tier1.name: Munters
tier1.strength: Global leader in aquaculture HVAC. Dedicated aquaculture division. Trusted by MOWI, Cermaq, SalMar.

tier1.name: Swegon
tier1.strength: Industrial HVAC with aquaculture adaptations. More cost-competitive than Munters.

tier2.name: Systemair
tier2.note: General industrial HVAC — can serve aquaculture but lacks specialist expertise.

tier2.name: Local Norwegian HVAC contractors
tier2.note: For installation and maintenance — not design/supply of specialist equipment.

categories.lock_in_flag: Munters systems are highly proprietary — service and spare parts locked to their ecosystem. Negotiate long-term service agreements at point of sale, not after installation.

categories.name: Electrical, SCADA & Automation
categories.description: Plant-wide electrical infrastructure, process control (SCADA), sensors, monitoring, MCC panels

tier1.name: ABB Norway
tier1.strength: Dedicated aquaculture automation division in Norway. Full SCADA capability. Strong local service.
tier1.risk: Proprietary DCS — lock-in risk if integrated with all systems

tier1.name: Siemens Norway
tier1.strength: Industrial SCADA, PLC and MCC expertise. Very strong in Norwegian industry.
tier1.risk: High lock-in — Siemens TIA Portal ecosystem is sticky

tier1.name: Schneider Electric
tier1.strength: EcoStruxure platform — more open architecture than competitors. Growing aquaculture footprint.

tier2.name: ScaleAQ (digital/software)
tier2.note: Aquaculture-native SCADA and production management software. Best-of-breed for fish production data — but lock-in risk is VERY high.

tier2.name: AquaCloud
tier2.note: Cloud-based aquaculture data platform. Open architecture — a way to reduce SCADA lock-in.

categories.lock_in_flag: ⚠️ HIGHEST LOCK-IN RISK IN CATEGORY. SCADA systems from any of these vendors become deeply embedded. Key ask: insist on open data protocols (OPC-UA), data portability guarantees, and avoid sole-source integration at contract stage.

categories.name: Oxygen & Gas Supply
categories.description: Liquid oxygen supply, oxygenation equipment, CO2 stripping systems — critical for fish survival in RAS

tier1.name: AGA (Linde Group)
tier1.strength: Dominant in Norwegian aquaculture. Nationwide logistics, reliable supply chain.
tier1.risk: Near-monopoly in some regions — limited negotiating leverage

tier1.name: Air Liquide
tier1.strength: Global scale, competitive on large-volume contracts.
tier1.risk: Logistics in Northern Norway can be challenging

tier2.name: Linde Gas
tier2.note: Part of same group as AGA — effectively limits choice in practice.

tier2.name: Yara Industrial
tier2.note: CO2 supply and industrial gases — relevant for CO2 stripping applications.

categories.lock_in_flag: Infrastructure lock-in: onsite tanks and pipework installed by AGA/Air Liquide create switching friction. Negotiate multi-year pricing caps at build stage. Consider onsite O2 generation (Oxymat, Novair) to reduce dependency for large facilities.

categories.name: Fish Feed Systems
categories.description: Automated feeding systems (hardware), feed silos, feed distribution, camera-assisted feeding control

tier1.name: AKVA Group (feed systems)
tier1.strength: Integrated with their RAS system. Seamless data flow. Market leader in Norway.
tier1.risk: Increases AKVA dependency if also RAS provider

tier1.name: Arvo-Tec
tier1.strength: Independent feed system specialist. Strong pellet-detection camera tech.
tier1.risk: Finnish — less local service in Norway

tier1.name: IFF/Intragilis
tier1.strength: Norwegian independent. Strong customer base among major salmon farmers.

tier2.name: ScaleAQ (feed systems)
tier2.note: Feed system integration with their software platform. Strong if ScaleAQ chosen for SCADA.

categories.lock_in_flag: Evaluate independently from RAS provider. Bundling feed systems with AKVA increases dependency. IFF or Arvo-Tec give independent optionality.

categories.name: Fish Feed Supply (Ongoing)
categories.description: Ongoing commercial fish feed — the largest operational cost category over plant lifetime
categories.spend_estimate: Largest single opex item — 50-60% of production cost

tier1.name: Skretting (Nutreco/SHV)
tier1.strength: Norway's largest feed supplier. Deep R&D. Norwegian salmon specialist.
tier1.risk: Premium pricing; may deprioritise smaller accounts

tier1.name: BioMar
tier1.strength: Strong sustainability credentials. Growing RAS-specific feed range.
tier1.risk: Slightly smaller Norwegian market share than Skretting

tier1.name: Ewos (Cargill)
tier1.strength: Global backing (Cargill). Competitive on price for larger volumes.

categories.lock_in_flag: None significant — this is a competitive market. Run a proper tender and secure multi-year pricing. Volume guarantees unlock 8–15% discount vs spot.

strategic_recommendation: Cermaq's primary risk in this supply market is over-dependence on AKVA Group. With AKVA already the RAS integrator (Hasvik contract), also awarding feed systems and SCADA creates a single-vendor ecosystem with limited exit options. Recommended strategy: (1) Appoint AKVA for RAS integration but insist on open-architecture SCADA and data portability in the contract. (2) Source feed systems independently (IFF or Arvo-Tec). (3) Engage Munters early for HVAC — 6–9 month lead time is a programme risk. (4) Run competitive tender for civil construction — capacity is the constraint, not capability. (5) Lock oxygen supply pricing at build stage via multi-year contract with capex for onsite generation feasibility review.
quick_wins: Engage Munters immediately — HVAC lead times will be your first programme risk
Negotiate open-architecture and data portability clauses into the AKVA contract before signing
Run civil construction tender now — Northern Norway contractor capacity is being absorbed by energy projects
Lock oxygen pricing — AGA/Air Liquide multi-year contract with capex for onsite O2 feasibility
